Land Rover Defender P400e Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender P400e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Defender P400e (PT204) engine typically costs £3,000–£5,800 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200–£2,000 depending on location and workshop rates. Note that hybrid battery replacement is a separate cost — typically £6,000–£10,000+ from a main dealer.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender P400e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P400e uses the PT204 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine, paired with a 105kW electric motor. The engine itself is generally robust, but cooling system issues — charge air cooler leaks, turbo coolant hose failures, and water pump problems — are well-documented across the Ingenium petrol range. The hybrid system adds complexity; electric coolant pump failures have also been reported.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Defender P400e?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Coolant leaks from the charge air cooler, turbo hoses, or water pump — the most frequently reported problem, (2) Electric coolant pump failure in the hybrid system, and (3) Rough idle or hesitation from the 2.0-litre petrol engine, particularly when cold or in hybrid mode.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender P400e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £45,000–£65,000 Defender P400e, a £4,000–£5,800 recon is around 10% of the car's value — worth it, particularly given Defender values have remained strong. However, if the hybrid battery also needs replacement (£6,000–£10,000+), evaluate total repair costs against the vehicle's value.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender P400e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P400e uses the PT204 (also known as AJ200P) — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine. The engine number is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the P400e coolant leak, or does it need engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — coolant leaks on the P400e are almost always repairable without engine replacement. Common fixes include charge air cooler replacement (£300–£800), turbo coolant hose repair (£200–£500), or water pump replacement (£400–£800). The key is catching the leak early — ignored coolant loss can lead to overheating and catastrophic engine damage.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Defender P400e Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender P400e engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Hybrid system calibration adds additional labour.

cta.label: → Compare Defender P400e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Coolant Leak (Charge Air Cooler / Turbo Hoses)
problems.symptoms: Coolant level dropping with no visible external leak, coolant smell from engine bay, low coolant warning light
problems.typicalMileage: 20,000–60,000 miles — can occur on low-mileage examples
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (charge air cooler replacement or hose repair)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant level drops frequently

problems.recommendation: Coolant loss is the most frequently reported issue on the P400e's 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine. Common failure points include the charge air cooler (internal leak at high revs), turbocharger coolant feed O-ring, and water pump. The charge air cooler leak is particularly insidious — coolant burns off through the exhaust without visible external signs. If you're topping up coolant regularly, book a pressure test immediately. A £300–£800 repair is far cheaper than the overheating damage that follows if ignored.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide → /failures/ingenium-coolant-leak/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Electric Coolant Pump Failure (Hybrid System)
problems.symptoms: Engine temperature rising rapidly, check engine light activation, sudden overheating warning
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: overheating can cause catastrophic engine damage

problems.recommendation: The P400e's hybrid cooling system relies on an electric coolant pump that can fail due to a faulty cable connection. This is a known issue shared with the Range Rover Sport Hybrid. If you see a temperature warning, stop driving immediately — continued driving with an overheating 2.0-litre Ingenium engine can cause head gasket failure or worse. The repair is often straightforward (cable replacement) once diagnosed correctly.

failureLink.label: Read the full electric coolant pump guide → /failures/electric-coolant-pump/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Rough Idle / Hesitation (2.0-litre Petrol Engine)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle when stationary, hesitation or stuttering at low speed / light throttle, especially when the engine is cold
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — reported on low-mileage examples
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: Multiple P400e owners have reported a rough idle and hesitation when the 2.0-litre engine is running, particularly when the hybrid battery is depleted or the engine is cold. Some describe it as "almost like the timing is out". This may be a characteristic of the four-cylinder Ingenium rather than a fault — but if it worsens or is accompanied by warning lights, get it checked. The P400e's 2.0-litre four-cylinder is notably less refined than the six-cylinder P400 petrol.

failureLink.label: Read the full rough idle guide → /failures/rough-idle-ingenium/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender P400e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Common repair on Ingenium petrol engines
Simple electrical fix if caught early
Often warranty-covered; assess cost vs vehicle value
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Defender P400e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with JLR-approved oil and coolant specs
* Evidence of regular use (hybrid batteries degrade with extended inactivity)
* Coolant system pressure test passed with no leaks
* Remaining manufacturer warranty (5-year / unlimited miles from new)
avoidIf: * Coolant warning light or frequent top-ups without documented fix
* Check engine light with no diagnosis — hybrid systems are complex
* No history of the 2.0-litre Ingenium's known coolant issues being addressed
* Low mileage with long periods of inactivity (hybrid battery health risk)

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Defender P400e is a sophisticated plug-in hybrid that pairs a 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor. It's the quickest Defender in the range (0-60 in 5.4 seconds) and offers up to 27–27 miles of electric-only range. However, the 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine has well-documented cooling system weaknesses — charge air cooler leaks, turbo coolant hose failures, and water pump issues. Buy with documented coolant system history, ideally with remaining manufacturer warranty, and you'll have a genuinely capable and rapid hybrid off-roader.

cta.label: → Compare Defender P400e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender P400e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rough idle or hesitation when the engine first runs
* Coolant Level — Check for frequent top-ups; smell for coolant in the engine bay
* Service History — Including the 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol (PT204) and confirmed coolant system inspections
* Hybrid Battery Health — Check electric-only range matches claimed figures (27 miles)
* Coolant System Pressure Test — Ask for evidence of a recent pressure test
* Charging System — Test both AC wallbox and rapid charging functionality
* Warning Lights — Any check engine or hybrid system warnings? Have codes been read?
* Warranty Status — The 5-year / unlimited miles warranty is valuable — check remaining cover
